title: Matrix access tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Matrix Synapse access token detected. Tokens prefixed with 'syt_' are Synapse homeserver access tokens that authenticate clients to the Matrix API. Exposing this token allows unauthorized access to read/send messages, join rooms, access user data, and impersonate the user on the Matrix network. Revoke the token immediately through the homeserver admin API and store tokens securely using environment variables or secrets management systems.

Matrix access token detected in configuration. Matrix access tokens are opaque strings used to authenticate with Matrix homeservers. These tokens grant full access to user accounts including reading messages, sending messages, joining rooms, and accessing private data. Exposing access tokens allows complete account takeover. Replace hardcoded tokens with environment variables or use a secrets manager (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).
